Title: Assessment and risk-based classification of data loss events in Sirius temperature loggers
Expected results of Action: Review the next 4 data loss events involving Sirius loggers to confirm correct classification and handling. Data loss events are consistently classified per defined framework.    No missed escalation of high-criticality events.
Action plan: NC428 Lost data on DIA169 temp logger due to low battery: identified a short period of missing temperature data from a Sirius logger. Although no product or sample impact was identified and incubator performance was stable before and after the event, the incident highlighted that not all data loss scenarios carry the same criticality. Currently, there is no formal risk-based framework defining the impact and required actions for different types of data loss. Review historical deviations and incidents involving Sirius logger data loss (e.g. last 24 months) to identify patterns, frequency, duration, and root causes of data loss. Confirm whether any previously reported events resulted in product or sample impact.     CategoryDescriptionImpactLow  Short data gap, stable trend before/after, redundant monitoring available  No impact  Medium  Longer data gap, no redundancy, stable equipment  Potential risk  High  Data loss during critical phase or unstable conditions  High risk    Assess known failure modes of Sirius loggers (e.g. battery, communication dropouts, memory overwrite). Whether data gaps can occur without loss of actual temperature control.    Depending on the assessment findings, update SOP4032 to include instructions on: Risk-based assessment of logger data loss  Documentation requirements per criticality level

Title: Test out different re-processing options for product BRA-4F1
Expected results of Action: The next produced batch of BRA-4F1 on purchase order #93293 should pass the quality criteria of 85% purity.

Title: Evaluation of alternatives or improvements to the nitrogen inventory database
Expected results of Action: This CAPA will be seen as successful if a decision to start implementing or improving a system for data logging of nitrogen tanks has been made by the end of Q3 2025.
Action plan: While SharePoint lists are effective for managing small-scale data entries, they encounter performance limitations when handling extensive datasets. Although a SharePoint list can accommodate up to 30 million items, practical usage often encounters challenges beyond 5,000 items due to the list view threshold, leading to potential performance issues. This limitation becomes particularly evident in scenarios requiring complex, multi-level hierarchical structures, such as the organization of nitrogen tanks (Tank → Rack → Box → Position). Consequently, users may experience difficulties in expanding and viewing all positions within a specific tank or rack, potentially leading to operational inefficiencies. This is identified as one of the root causes in NC366. Additionally, managing historical data within SharePoint can be challenging. For instance, accidental deletions, such as the removal of a location in the cell stock, have necessitated external intervention to restore information by contacting Braathe (NC191), highlighting potential vulnerabilities in data integrity.This CAPA aims to evaluate alternative solutions for nitrogen storage logging to enhance scalability, data integrity, and user accessibility. The evaluation will encompass, but is not limited to, the following options:- Enhancing SharePoint Lists: Investigate potential improvements through customization to better support large datasets and complex hierarchical structures.- Integration with Other Microsoft Applications: Explore exporting data to applications such as PowerApps, which offer customizable data models and may provide improved performance and flexibility.- Adoption of Specialized Software: Consider implementing software solutions specifically designed for nitrogen storage or similar inventory systems, which may offer tailored functionalities and better performance.- Utilization of the NOVA Platform: Assess the feasibility of leveraging the existing NOVA platform license to manage nitrogen storage logging, evaluating its capabilities in handling complex data structures and large volumes.Evaluation Criteria:Each alternative will be assessed based on the following criteria:- Cost: Initial investment, licensing fees, and long-term maintenance expenses.- Ease of Data Transfer: Complexity and reliability of migrating existing data to the new system.- Data Integrity: Ability to maintain accurate and secure data records, including robust backup and recovery mechanisms.- User Interface: Intuitiveness and efficiency of the user interface, facilitating ease of use and reducing the learning curve for personnel.   The outcome of this evaluation will provide a comprehensive overview of the benefits and challenges associated with each approach, guiding the selection of the most suitable solution for nitrogen storage inventory management.

Title: Evaluation of suboptimal storage temperature on ADCF medium
Expected results of Action: NA
Action plan: Test medium stored at DHL over 5 days to conclude on usability. compare to medium stored at correct temperature.   parameters that should be considered: Chemical Instability: Some medium components (e.g., amino acids, vitamins, growth factors) may degrade outside the recommended temperature range. Test glutamine, ammonia, glucose and lactate on BioAnalyser.  Precipitation: Certain salts or supplements might crystallize or precipitate at 0°C, altering medium composition. Perform visual inspection.  Microbial Contamination: Temperatures above 8°C could accelerate microbial growth if contamination occurs. Aseptically perform a microbial growth test  (streak out on plates + 50 ml in tubes) according to normal regime.  Product Performance: The functionality of the medium may be affected, leading to suboptimal cell growth. Perform test productions.    Testing should be performed according to an approved protocol, and usabiltiy of medium concluded in a report. Only if medium is considered to be ok to use, can it be released from quaranteen.

Title: Replace incubation time with substrate during ELISA with optical density value
Expected results of Action: There will be no recurring NCs related to the exceeding of the incubation time with substrate for atleast 10 assays following the implementation of this CAPA.
Action plan: The Diagnostic production facility works with several clones and isotypes, and the reaction of enzymes used in ELISA differ for each of clones. SOP 1007 version 5.0 states incubation of ELISA plates with substrate for 10-60 minutes. Even though this time frame works well with most of the ELISA performed, some exceptions cannot be excluded. This time frame is specified to obtain the desired absorbance signal (O.D.= 0.8- 1.2) from the samples after incubatinon. For some antibodies (especially anti-human IgE for example), it is quite common to incubate over 60 minutes to get desired absorbance signal. The current instruction in the SOP is quite strict and does not allow any flexibility to incubate for longer if the antibody being tested requires this extra time to reach the desired absorbance. This leads to several unnecessary non- conformances.   Having a required absorbance signal also avoid non-conformances which can occur due to incubation for shorter than 10 minutes. In some of the cases, for eg. dilution of conjugate other than the recommended dilution, can differ the required incubation period. Having the absorbance signal value instead of time frame optimizes the process to achieve better results for calculation. Several new clones are added as well in the production facility, so optimization of this procedure with O.D. value also prevents future deviations.    This change of the incubation period only applies for SOP1007. Other types of ELISA performed in the lab has specific requirements for incubation which cannot be changed.   CAPA138 is already opened for major revision of SOP1007 and the incubation period on the SOP will be updated during this revision.    The proposed CAPA is to: Rewrite the instruction in the SOP1007 step 9.9.3 to allow the analyst to monitor the ELISA plate to ensure the desired absorbance is within (O.D.= 0.8- 1.2) is reached in this step, rather than specifying the time that the plate should be incubated for, in step 9.9.3.  If required, a statement can be added to the new version of the SOP with wording as follows  A guidance time limits of 10-60 minutes is recommended in most cases, however specific samples may require incubation times outside these limits to reach the required absorbance. The time taken in this incubation for the sample to reach the desired absorbance will continue to be documented in the control form for the analysis, as this will provide useful information for future tests and provide better understanding of the product performance in relation to the method, and for optimising and planning future analysis.  "
Description: Effectiveness check is performed after 7 assays following the implementation of this CAPA. No non-conformances related to incubation time has been opened. Due to low frequency of the analytical assays the time needed for 10 assays is impractical. 7 assays are considered to be sufficient to evaluate the effectiveness of this CAPA.

Title: Update SOP0074 with troubleshooting instructions

Nothing is mentioned regarding guidelines and troubleshooting when using Protein L and pH 3.0-3.5 is not working. SOP0005 Affinity purification of immunoglobulins by FPLC Protein A and L mentions protein L but is more specific towards protein A. SOP0074 must be updated regarding guidelines for Protein L purifications, instruction for troubleshooting when indicated pH is not working. It must also be described how to handle these elution issues and who to inform/contact. SOP0005 should be updated for more clear structure regarding protein L. Consider to move all relevant protein L information to SOP0074. Training must be given to operators in the updated processes.
Description: Operator Gro Stokke (GS) left the company in June 2024. GS was trained in SOP0074 and updates regarding CAPA, but did not perform any protein L purification after last training.  Operator Suyog Basnet (SBA) did perform SOP0074 purification procedure after training. He was aware of possible pH issues and how to find info regarding troubleshooting. Still he has not yet experienced needed use of troubleshooting on his own.

Title: Update Flow cytometry process with detailed instructions regarding temp, time and general handling of samples and sample preparations
Expected results of Action: This CAPA is considered effective if 3 batches analysed after completion of this CAPA are confirmed to have proper cell separation and gating of cell populations. Lymphocytes, monocytes and granulocytes correspond to expected outcome compared to previously approved batches. Each batch consists of both fraction and QC analysis.
Action plan: Update Flow cytometry process with factors for maintaining better sample integrity. Following factors must be included: -        Appropriate storage temperature, samples are to be kept on crushed ice at all times when not being handled. -        As short time as possible the pellets without supernatant during staining procedure. perform quick suction of supernatant and resuspend cells in solution quickly after. No need to resuspend cells too much. No need to remove all supernatant. It is also important to note that the complexity of performing the analysis increases with number of samples, requiring an experienced operator. -        Recommended to reduce the time needed to run all the samples Evaluate if a diagram of gating performed should be included with the analysis. Procedure should be updated in collaboration with VFA.

Title: Update SOP0001 to include the possibility to add extra FBS in medium if needed.
Expected results of Action: This CAPA plan is created to align the instructions in SOP to match current practice, to be able to culture more difficult clones and ensure a more robust but flexible procedure. As this is an improvement to the documentation to adhere to current practice, it does not require an effectiveness check.
Action plan: SOP0001 is to be updated to include the possibility to add extra FBS as needed, either based on experience or in agreement with production manager. Justification: SOP0001 states that standard medium for culturing of hybridomas is RPMI 1640 supplemented with 10% fetal bovine serum (FBS). SOP0001 also states that the operators are responsible to check specifications in product specification (PS) to ensure correct medium and supplements are used. Previous experience shows that certain cell lines needs extra FBS to grow, and it has been a common practice to add extra FBS where needed. As an improvement to the current SOP, adding this possibility will increase robustnes and flexibility of the process, while ensuring the viability and therefore production of more difficult clones.

Title: Open supplier complaint regarding recent CL1000 issues
Expected results of Action: The supplier changed the manufacturing process of the critical consumable (membrane) used in CL1000 flasks, which was identified as the root cause of the observed leakages. The flasks with the new membranes were evaluated by the supplier, and their performance was found to be satisfactory (refer to report SCR070). Additionally, Curida Diatec conducted an internal study to evaluate the effectiveness of the supplier’s correction (feasibility study DEV164). During this study, six flasks were used in the internal production of two clones. No leakage or contamination was observed when the new flasks were used. What is more, the flasks performed satisfactorily in the upstream production process with both used cell lines. The flasks with the new membrane were implemented via change control CMID80 on 10.10.2024.
Action plan: Register a supplier complaint regarding the CELLine 1000 units with lot 0000035023 which have been suffering from membrane leakage between the outer medium compartment and the cell compartment.   A total of 42 units of CL1000 flasks with lot 0000035023 was received, of which 4 (10%) so far have been confirmed to suffer from membrane leakage of cells. This has caused the production timeline to suffer and in case of NC147 requiring a new startup of CL1000 production unit.
Description: The investigation conducted by the supplier is considered satisfactory. The CL1000 flasks with the new membranes performed well, and no leakage or contamination was observed when used by the operator in July–August 2024.

Title: Monitoring possible technical issues identified at change of Protein A media
Expected results of Action: Equipment monitoring regarding pressure should be logged over a period of 6 months. Use of new Protein a media regarding pressure should be approvable if pressure limits are acceptable, and purification runs are not interrupted due to high pressure buildup.  Criteria for control of issue is monitoring of two different clones to be purified in large scale, and not more than previous observed precipitation as result.
Action plan: Monitor pressure at large column runs using new Protein A media. High pressure may appear at scale-up using new Protein A  media, when processing large scale products. At time of report current large scale column size is 400mL, diameter 5cm (height 20cm) and flow speed 45mL/min and maximum pressure value of 0.8 mPa, not to be exceeded. It is acceptable to operate at higher pressure in pumps as long as pressure over the column is not more than 0.8mPa and rest of pressure buildup is over tubing and switches.   Monitor precipitation in large scale products after switch to new Protein A media. A Possibility of precipitation issues in larger scale products during purification and dialysis is identified in DEV163 report. Acceptable results will be not more observed precipitationthan than in previous batches.

Title: Establish new SDS page technical requirements in SOP1066
Expected results of Action: 90% of SDS runs performed in 2 months after an action is executed will be correctly interpreted (verified) by an operator. The reference to all evaluated SDS runs has to be provided in the action description.
Action plan: Currently, technical criteria for the SDS run are defined according to SOP1066:10.1.1 The Standard pattern must be according to the product inlet from the supplier. If more bands are detected, the extra bands must not interfere with the 10 main bands. The intensity of extra bands must be lower than the 10 main bands.  10.1.2 Duplicates of test sample must be comparable.  10.1.3 10 bands must be detected for the standard.  10.1.4 The most intense band of the standard should be less intense than the most intense band of the sample.  After reconsideration of the technical requirements for SOP1066 BIORAD Stainfree SDS, point 10.1.4 and 10.1.3 do not seem to reflect the quality of the assay and do not serve its intended purpose.  10.1.4   The most intense band of the standard should be less intense than the most intense band of the sample." The requirement is often difficult to approve since the analysis is not a quantitative one, hence it relies on visual inspection of the analysis. The SOP1066 clearly defines both concentrations of the sample and volume loaded to the precast gel. This concentration leads to an intensity of the sample band that borders the most intense band on the standard, it can also be difficult to determine if the bands are more intense or just thicker. This can lead to unnecessary rejection of QC analysis, as the quality of the product is not affected by the intensity of the band, and SDS-PAGE analysis is used for purity analysis..  10.1.3 10 bands must be detected for the standard. This is a possible contradiction to point 10.1.1 (which includes the possibility of more bands as long as it is according to the supplier's specifications) and should be rewritten into point 10.1.1. Therefore it is suggested to update SOP1066 where new SDS criteria is established and point 10.1.3 is re-written within point 10.1.1 and point 10.1.4 is removed. At the same time, it should be considered if other requirements might be better for the approval of SDS analysis."

Title: Update CAPA  process in BizzMine, with adding technical verification and additional QA approval steps
Expected results of Action: 80 % of CAPA, which will be approved six months after this CAPA execution, will have property-defined evidence of execution.
Action plan: Reason: Several CAPAs were revised in November 2022, and the following observation was raised. Major CAPA was not well defined (unspecific wording), and the expected results were written so that it's hard to evaluate the effectiveness of the action, evidence of execution was not given. Plan: CAPA plans to update the CAPA process in BizzMine by adding a step of technical verification for the CAPA Plan. QA Approval was added to each main process step: after CAPA Plan, CAPA Execution, and CAPA effectiveness check. The SOP will be updated to define how CAPA evidence should be reported.
Description: Since the SOP5039 was updated, 77 CAPAs were registered and technically verified for execution. From that CAPA, ten did not have defined acceptance criteria for effectiveness evaluation. Therefore, 87% of registered and technical verified CAPA had well-defined criteria for their effectiveness evaluation. The CAPA effectiveness evaluation criteria were evaluated: - All have a defined period when they have to be evaluated (period, number of runs, number of produced batches) - All have countable criteria that can be assessed (number of on-time executed training, number of failed runs, reoccurring events, collected feedback from employees, etc.).  See attachment 1.

Title: The SDS and ELISA procedures have to be updated to include instructions on the type of material used when the reference batch is unavailable
Expected results of Action: Clear instruction are provided in SOP in the relevant SOPs
Action plan: Several analytical methods used in quality and in-process control often require that the reference batch is run together with the test batch. This rule applies to techniques such as ELISA and BIORAD Stainfree SDS. In case when product is produced for the first time, and there is no available reference material/batch, it is allowed to use the same isotype as a reference batch. Until now, these were informal rules in the production, when a new test production was started. Example is attached. The SDS and ELISA procedures have to be updated to include instructions on the type of material used when the reference batch is unavailable.
Description: The correct, approved batch was used in all the SDS analyses performed within 6 months after the CAPA was executed. There was no similar deviation to NCR51 reported in the last 6 months. Therefore, CAPA is considered effective.

Description: The root couase was identified by C3 company to sucontractor/assembing. Action was taken internally at C3, the CAPA-22-001 was created internally and completed in June 22. Curida Diatec did not experienced similar issue with flow path in one year after complain was filled.

Title: Update SOP4045 to take temperature under consideration while working with freezer
Expected results of Action: No similar incident reoccurs during next stock count.
Action plan: Update SOP4045 to take temperature under consideration while working with freezer to prevent temperature going beyond the alarm limit.
Description: The stock count was performed in December 2023. The temperature was not exceeded during that operation. The temperature monitoring data are attached

Title: Update the local procedure SOP 5011 how to handle the scenario when the overproduced product is sold to a different customer.
Expected results of Action: The explicit instruction is documented on how to handle this scenario. All the overproduced products, sold to different customers, are handled in this same way according to updated procedure.
Action plan: Reason: For the products that Diatec has a license to produce, this same batch can be sold to several customers. If a product was produced according to a specific PS, and it was over-produced, the stock can be sold later to other customers. This option is only applicable to the CD markers as the customers own the other clones. The practice has been that new customer has been informed regarding product specification (without including the other customer names). A new customer had to confirm that a product meets their requirements by email. The new Product Specification is not created. The new COA for that case is prepared without the customer's name. This scenario is not secured by the local procedure, as there is no information regarding this in SOP5011. It should be evaluated if it is better to make 2 COA for these products, one for the specific customer order and one for Diatec to have in stock.  Plan: Update the local procedure SOP 5011 how to handle the scenario when the overproduced product is sold to a different customer.
Description: The effectiveness evaluation is considered effective. In addition to that action 2 following improvements were made. The new COA template was approved on 6.11.2024, and the customer name is no longer included in the document. The stock overview is maintained in the NOVA system implemented in November 2023, which provides an overview of the stock. All overproduced stock can be sold easily to customer when needed.

Title: Evaluate need of use reference batch in GPC-HPLC method
Expected results of Action: The Quality Control of the next batch of Product 9980 and 9990 will be executed correctly.
Action plan: It should be evaluated if the testing of the reference batch is beneficial or critical for the Quality Control of the NGAL products (9980 and 9990). The change control must be initiated if the reference batch should be excluded from the testing regime. The external laboratory conformed that all tested samples are compared with the refernce spectrum as a part of Method of Analyse AM-431 ” Purity of monomeric antibody by gel-permaeation chromatography (GPC-HPLC) Diatec/Abbot” If will be decided that the reference batch should be kept as a control for the analysis AM-431, both control plans have to be updated with details on how the sample should be prepared for analysis and how to interpreter/report results for reference batch. Documents which has to be updated: K2C-9980 nGAL 1-903-430 v.05 and Control Plan K2C-9990 nGAL 1-2322-455
Description: The GPC-HPLC analysis was performed for the following batches B8786, B8787, B8788, B8783, B8784, and B8785. All batches were sampled correctly for analysis. No issue related to analysis run was reported.

Title: Converted document used for QA approval (huskeliste") into official document"
Expected results of Action: No more than one customer complaint will reported related to COA content in 4 months after CAPA is executed.
Action plan: The “Huslkeliste” is the uncontrolled, unofficial document which QA uses during the approval process of the masterbatch record. This document is not controlled and not dilated enough to catch all errors in the documentation before product approval.  The quality assurance approval process has to be guarded by the procedure. “Huskleliste” should be established as an official document. The potential of using BizzMine software should be evaluated if it can be beneficial to use it in that process.
Description: There were no customer complaints reported reagrding wrong information on COA in 4 months since new procedure was implemented.

Title: Redesign Certificate of analysis (COA) and Product Specifications (PS)
Action plan: Creating Certificate of analysis (COA) and Product Specifications (PS) have to be redesigned to minimize the number of errors in the documentation. Creating COA requires manually transferring data from PS and worksheets into the COA. Often previously approved COA is used as a template for new documents.  Several errors were found during document revision or by the customers (refer to CCR075 and CCR0075). Therefore, the procedure for creating COA documents has to be reviewed. The potential of using BizzMine software should be evaluated if it can benefit of using it for that process.
Description: Since the COA template was updated in November 2024, 28 batches have been approved and released using the new COA template. No customer complaints were registered during this five-month period regarding incorrect information in the COA.Therefore, the CAPA is considered effective.
